element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Final gauntlet with all mechanics combined

typical_relief_triggers: Completing a world and returning to overworld map
Finding safe zones with power-up reserves
Reaching checkpoints or fortress completion markers
typical_tension_triggers: Entering fortress levels with no power-up
Giant Land obstacles at doubled scale
Time running low during timed levels
Entering unfamiliar world with new enemy types
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty escalation across 8 worlds with tension peaks at fortress/airship boss encounters. Early worlds emphasize mechanic introduction with Safe/Moderate contexts; mid-game introduces thematic variation (Desert, Giant, Sky); late-game World 8 combines all mechanics with heightened danger. Relief provided by overworld map breaks and bonus mini-games between action stages.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Overworld map pathways branch into multiple routes to same world goal, allowing players to skip certain fortresses or choose difficulty via path selection. Side paths lead to bonus mini-games and Toad Houses offering optional power-up access.
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Vines hidden behind tiles and breakable bricks; upward exploration in tall chambers rewarded with coins and power-ups; pipes provide vertical shortcuts and hidden passages

difficulty_curve.early_game: Worlds 1-2 introduce core mechanics (running, jumping, stomping) with basic Goomba/Koopa enemies. Super Leaf and slope mechanics appear but remain optional. Difficulty primarily from spatial precision rather than enemy density.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Worlds 3-5 introduce thematic environmental challenges (water levels, desert obstacles, giant-scaled enemies). Fortress levels present continuous enemy gauntlets. First Boom Boom encounters demand pattern recognition. Koopaling airship bosses introduce mechanical difficulty.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Worlds 6-7 combine multiple mechanic types within single levels. Auto-scrolling airship sequences reduce player control. Tank levels introduce time pressure. World 8 culminates with all obstacles present simultaneously before final Bowser castle gauntlet.
notable_spikes: Fortress levels first appearance (transition from action panels to enemy gauntlets)
World 4 Giant Land (scale inversion creates spatial disorientation)
First Koopaling airship boss (introduces timed pattern-recognition boss mechanic)
World 8 lead-up with tank and ship auto-scrolling sequences (reduced agency and time pressure)
Bowser's Castle final stage (all mechanics combined with highest enemy density)

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard horizontal scroll level with enemies, platforms, and power-ups distributed along left-to-right progression. Introduces new enemy types or mechanics in isolated, safe contexts.
Vertical or multi-directional indoor level with high enemy density, Boom Boom mini-boss encounter, and limited power-ups. Tests mastery of core mechanics against sustained enemy pressure.
World-specific obstacle type (desert quicksand, water currents, giant platforms) combined with appropriate enemy roster. Teaches world-specific mechanic mastery.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: World 2 desert levels, World 3 sky levels, World 4 giant-scaled levels

notable_level_patterns.description: Reduced player horizontal control with level scrolling at fixed rate. Forces timing and reaction rather than exploration. High danger zones with fewer safe platforms.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: World 8 tank levels, World 8 airship sequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Multi-stage encounter with flying airship theme. Boss descends with predictable pattern; player must dodge and counter-attack. Reward is world progression and fortress removal from map.
Optional overworld map encounter (card matching, slot machine, etc.) offering power-up rewards. Tests luck or pattern memory; completely optional progression.
